Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons On Ground Of Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction Or To Stay Action On Ground Of Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar May-18-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. MALCOLM LEADER-PICONE, TEL. NO. 1-510-444-2404 X 24 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JAY WITHEE OF HARTNETT, SMITH & PAETKAU, TEL. NO. 1-650-260-1259 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court adopted its tentative rulings with modification. DEFENDANT JOYCE LIVESTOCK COMPANY'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons On Ground Of Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction Or To Stay Action On Ground Of Forum Non Conveniens is Denied. Defendant availed itself of California benefits by retaining a California law firm with natural resources law expertise most often developed by California firms for a seven-year representation. During that representation, the large majority of the legal work was performed in California. Defendant made hundreds of contacts with California in consultations during the representation by teleconference and e-mail. Convenience is also served by a California forum, as the large majority of witnesses (lawyers of worked on the case) and the billing records are here. Defendant shall have 20 days from the date of Entry of Order, denying Defendant's motion within which to file a petition for a writ of mandamus challenging the order, or file a responsive pleading. Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter; Rose Miller, CSR #5041

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons On Ground Of Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction Or To Stay Action On Ground Of Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Apr-27-2016 Continued To May-18-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

DEFENDANT JOYCE LIVESTOCK COMPANY'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons On Ground Of Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction Or To Stay Action On Ground Of Forum Non Conveniens is continued to May 18, 2016 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302 by agreement of the parties.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
